items.headline: Netherlands Keukenhof draws global visitors for spring display
items.summary: Netherlands opened its Keukenhof garden in Lisse for the 2026 spring season, welcoming visitors to an eight-week display featuring around seven million flowers.

items.headline: Cameroon sees mass turnout for Pope Leo XIV visit as he calls for peace
items.summary: Cameroon saw thousands of faithful gather on Wednesday 16 April at Bamenda’s airfield, where Pope Leo XIV celebrated an open‑air Mass and called for a “decisive change of course” towards peace.

items.headline: Congo's Sassou-N'Guesso sworn in to fifth presidential term
items.summary: Denis Sassou-N’Guesso was sworn in on Thursday to a fifth term as president of the Republic of the Congo.

items.headline: 1,100 Kenyan workers face lay off as Meta ends contract with Sama
items.summary: After years of criticism and lawsuits, Meta is ending a major contract with the Kenyan outsourcing company Sama. The move will see more than 1,100 people lose their jobs, Sama said in a statement.

items.summary: Nagaissen Valaydum has been removed from his position as Chairperson of the Trust Fund for Excellence in Sports (TFES) following allegations of mismanagement and friction with board objectives. items.headline: "Sub-Saharan Africa resilient, despite mounting global shocks" - Abebe Selassie
items.summary: Sub-Saharan African economies have demonstrated remarkable resilience in the face of repeated global shocks, according to Abebe Aemro Selassie, Director of the African Department at the IMF.

items.headline: Storm Alert: 3 Regions Facing Severe Floods and Thunderstorms until 8 PM
items.summary: Heavy rain and thunderstorms are set to sweep across Mauritius today, with a formal weather watch in effect until 8:00 pm this Friday.
